questions.question: Who owned the unusual garden described in the poem?
Why is Mrs. Jones’s garden described as unusual?
questions.answer: Because everything in the garden is extremely small.

questions.question: What type of path is mentioned in the garden?
What water feature is present in the garden?
Which object in the garden tells time?
What is the rockery mentioned in the poem?
questions.answer: A small arrangement of stones in the garden.

questions.question: What did the narrator ask Mrs. Jones about?
questions.answer: About a flower in the garden.

questions.question: Why was the narrator unable to see the flower?
Where was the flower located?
What surprised the narrator at the end of the poem?
questions.answer: He realized he was standing on the flower.

questions.question: What feeling does Mrs. Jones show toward her garden?
Why is the poem considered humorous?
questions.answer: Because of the exaggerated description of the tiny garden.

questions.question: What literary device exaggerates the garden’s tiny features?
What does the narrator initially think about the garden?
What type of poem is 'A Concrete Example'?
What helps readers imagine the tiny garden clearly?
Why is the narrator confused in the poem?
questions.answer: Because he cannot locate the flower.

questions.question: What lesson does the narrator learn from the garden?
questions.answer: Small things can easily be overlooked.

questions.question: What idea about nature is presented in the poem?
questions.answer: Nature can be interesting even in small forms.

questions.question: What makes the ending of the poem surprising?
questions.answer: The flower was beneath the narrator’s feet.

questions.question: What does the poem encourage readers to observe?
Why does Mrs. Jones describe the garden carefully?
questions.answer: Because she is proud of her miniature garden.

questions.question: What tone dominates the poem?
What does the title 'A Concrete Example' suggest?
questions.answer: An example used to clearly explain an idea.

questions.question: What is the overall message of the poem?
questions.answer: Even tiny things can be meaningful and interesting.
